_meta.description: Device-side quirks confirmed empirically on Electra One MK2 firmware 4.1.4 (verified 2026-05-25). These are NOT in the official docs, or contradict them in subtle ways. Patterns flagged here usually cause silent failures (widget renders as blue-bar fallback, callbacks not firing, MIDI traffic missing) with no error message.

gotchas.title: graphics.drawText is not a real API
gotchas.symptom: Widget renders as a default blue fader on device with no error in the browser console.
gotchas.cause: graphics.drawText() raises 'attempt to call a nil value (field drawText)' at the first paint. Only graphics.print(x, y, text, width, alignment) exists.
gotchas.fix: Replace every drawText call with graphics.print(x, y, text, 9999, LEFT) (or pass a real width + CENTER/RIGHT alignment).

gotchas.title: Graphics calls require integer coordinates
gotchas.symptom: Paint aborts midway with 'bad argument #N to fillRect (number has no integer representation)'.
gotchas.cause: Any expression with a `/N` division (or implicit float) produces a float; graphics.fillRect / drawRect / drawLine / print / fillCircle reject non-integer coords.
gotchas.fix: Wrap every coord in math.floor(...). Theme.rect / outline / line in our shipped theme.lua already do this internally — only direct graphics.* calls need it.

gotchas.title: Pass 0xRRGGBB colours (24-bit), the firmware converts to RGB565 itself
gotchas.symptom: Colours render blue/cyan instead of the expected slate/terracotta palette.
gotchas.cause: Pre-converting a 24-bit colour to RGB565 (0x10C3 etc.) makes the firmware re-interpret the 16-bit number as a 24-bit colour, mapping into the lower 16 bits → mostly blue.
gotchas.fix: Keep colours as 0xRRGGBB in the source. The firmware release notes for 4.1.4 specifically fixed this conversion.

gotchas.title: Theme/primitives wrapped in (function() ... end)() lose their upvalues
gotchas.symptom: Paint/touch/pot callbacks don't fire on device; tile renders as blue-bar fallback. Often a single tile renders OK but a second one doesn't.
gotchas.cause: Closures defined inside an IIFE wrap reference upvalues that the firmware does not preserve after the chunk completes execution. Detected during phase 4-A bundling work.
gotchas.fix: Flat concatenation. theme.lua uses `Theme = Theme or {}` (not local Theme = {}), strip the trailing `return Theme`, primitives rewrite `local function NAME(...)` → `function Theme.NAME(...)`. See scripts/bundle-preset.js.

gotchas.title: `local g = graphics` at module top level breaks paint dispatch
gotchas.symptom: Custom tile renders as blue-bar fallback despite the bundle parsing fine.
gotchas.cause: Caching the graphics table as an upvalue creates a closure binding that the firmware drops after the chunk loads.
gotchas.fix: Reference `graphics` directly inside every helper. Never cache it.

gotchas.title: setPaintCallback / setTouchCallback / setPotCallback need global functions, not closures
gotchas.symptom: Widget renders once (the initial paint) but no further repaints; touches/pot rotations produce nothing.
gotchas.cause: Passing a local closure with upvalues to setPaintCallback causes the firmware to lose the binding after the load chunk returns.
gotchas.fix: Declare paintLane / touchLane / potLane as top-level globals. Keep per-tile state in global tables indexed by ctrl:getId().

gotchas.title: Only one pot is dispatched to a custom tile, regardless of how many `inputs` entries you declare
gotchas.symptom: You declare 2 inputs in the JSON expecting 2 pots, but only 1 pot triggers the setPotCallback AND produces USB MIDI traffic; the other declared pots are silent on both channels.
gotchas.cause: The `inputs[]` array is honoured for ADSR/fader/etc., but the firmware ignores it for type:'custom' tiles. See forum thread #4172 — Martin Pavlas confirmed multi-input custom is a planned feature, not shipped.
gotchas.fix: Plan UX around 1 pot per custom tile. Use single-click toggles + double-click for secondary actions, plus touchscreen tap/drag for navigation/editing.

gotchas.title: Press on a pot is not distinguishable from rotation start
gotchas.symptom: Tracking a 'click without rotation' is unreliable — the user has to be very gentle to avoid an accidental tiny delta.
gotchas.cause: events.onPotTouchChange fires on capacitive touch but doesn't distinguish press from turn. No onPotClick exists. Forums #4116 and #4185 confirm.
gotchas.fix: Track a `rotatedDuringTouch` flag (set true on MOVE, reset on DOWN), treat UP-without-MOVE as click. Use the touchscreen for clear DOUBLECLICK gestures instead — those ARE dispatched as a native event type.

gotchas.title: ev.id in setPotCallback is 0-indexed (0..11), even though the doc says potId 1..12
gotchas.symptom: Filter `ev.id ~= 1` never matches when the user turns the top-left pot.
gotchas.cause: The doc (presetformat.md) consistently calls them 'potId 1..12', but the Lua callback receives 0..11.
gotchas.fix: Use 0..11 in your filter logic. If you map JSON inputs (1..12) to Lua filters, subtract 1.

gotchas.title: The pot dispatched to a custom tile is on the OPPOSITE physical row
gotchas.symptom: User expects 'top pot controls top tile' but the top pot triggers the bottom tile and vice versa.
gotchas.cause: Empirical observation on firmware 4.1.4: tile in slotId 0 (top half of screen) receives ev.id=6 (= leftmost pot of the BOTTOM physical row). Tile in slotId 6 receives ev.id=0 (= leftmost pot of the TOP physical row).
gotchas.fix: Cross-dispatch in your potCallback: when sourceCtrl:getId()==1, operate on lanes[2]; when ==2, operate on lanes[1]. See widgets/note-list-16/widget.lua.

gotchas.title: slotId is 0-indexed (0..35 on the MK2 6×6 grid)
gotchas.symptom: Tile appears one column to the right of where you expected.
gotchas.cause: slotId=0 is top-left, slotId=5 is top-right of row 1, slotId=6 is the leftmost slot of row 2, etc.
gotchas.fix: Use 0..35 for MK2 6×6 layout. Note that `slotId` is not documented in presetformat.md — the canonical positioning field is `bounds: [x, y, w, h]`. slotId is an editor convenience that may be silently ignored on certain firmware paths.

gotchas.title: `span: "6"` reliably enlarges only the FIRST custom tile; the second is rendered as a single slot
gotchas.symptom: Two custom tiles each declaring span:6 — the first fills its row, the second is 146×56 in the middle of the second row.
gotchas.fix: Call ctrl:setBounds({0, y, w, h}) explicitly for every tile in preset.onLoad, instead of relying on span/vspan. The Lua setBounds is honoured for the visual paint area.

gotchas.title: Touch events route by slotId, not by setBounds
gotchas.symptom: Tap on the visible area of a tile selects nothing — hitCell returns nil.
gotchas.cause: If you reposition a tile visually via setBounds (e.g., swap top↔bottom), the firmware still dispatches touches based on the original slotId. event.x/event.y arrive in the original slot's coordinate space, but ctrl:getBounds() returns your overridden bounds — the hit test misses.
gotchas.fix: Pick a slotId that matches the target visual position; only use setBounds to ENLARGE (e.g., make a 1-slot tile cover a full row), not to MOVE.

gotchas.title: Device JSON needs `instrumentId: "generic-controls"`
gotchas.symptom: The preset uploads but the editor or device fails to recognize the device entry.
gotchas.fix: Always include `instrumentId: "generic-controls"` in each entry of devices[]. The default is implicit but explicit is safer.

gotchas.title: Mixing schemaVersion 2 and 3 in the same preset disables UI controls
gotchas.symptom: The editor shows the preset but the Edit/Upload buttons are missing.
gotchas.cause: The Firestore-doc-level `schemaVersion` field and the project-level `schemaVersion` field must match. A doc-level 3 with a v2-shaped project loses the UI controls.
gotchas.fix: Keep them in sync. The XT envelopes preset is a known-working v2 reference (id GK6wmbgvwM6S3GanpoN7) for custom-tile structure.

gotchas.title: The device logger is disabled by default — print() and fatal-error traces are silent until enabled
gotchas.symptom: Nothing appears in the editor's Lua log window when the widget runs.
gotchas.cause: 'For performance reasons' per the docs. The 4.1.4 firmware added a fatal-error handler that prints stack traces to the logger — but only if you turn the logger on.
gotchas.fix: In the editor's Lua tab, toggle the logger ON. Once enabled, print() output and fatal-error stack traces flow through.

gotchas.title: parameterMap.onChange is a global function you DEFINE, not one you call
gotchas.symptom: `attempt to call a nil value (field 'onChange')` at preset.onLoad.
gotchas.cause: Like preset.onLoad / events.onPageChange, the firmware invokes a global function you've declared. There is no parameterMap.onChange(vo, callback) registration API.
gotchas.fix: Declare `function parameterMap.onChange(valueObjects, origin, midiValue) ... end` at module scope. The firmware will invoke it whenever any ParameterMap entry changes.
